description: Overall, my experience with the AWS Management Console has been great. I love its powerful and centralized interface, which makes it easy to manage multiple services like EC2 and S3 in one place. The built-in monitoring tools and resource organization features are particularly useful, and switching between regions is seamless. It’s a robust platform that simplifies cloud resource management effectively.
author: Qazim A.
pros: The User Interface: The dashboard is allows me to navigate through services quickly.
cons: It can be challenging to locate specific features or services without prior experience, especially with the frequent updates.

description: I love the AWS Management Console. There are still some bugs the team need to work on. For example, when signed in, sometimes, the first URL request never loads so you have to refresh the page twice. Some CloudFront distribution graphs also break or reset if you're trying to do anything complex. I still love AWS.
author: Abdul Rehman M.
pros: The AWS Management Console was updated a while back with new UI and it has been so much easier to use since. There are updates daily on the UI almost I can tell that a LOT of effort went into this. The simple and clean design makes it so easy to provision and manage most AWS resources.
cons: I dislike how naming can sometimes be a little weird. For example, if you set up an AWS EC2 instance on the console, it will name the VPC using weird names which aren't bad but it can be confusing because the VPC then has an internal name and a display name.

review_title: A steep learning curve, yet powerful for navigating the AWS Service Library
description: From the perspective of a smaller business and a solo AWS administrator without prior experience, engaging with AWS infrastructure has been a steep learning curve but never fails in fascinating me with the never ending array of services and complex architectures that can be achieved. Coming from a Windows On-prem background the security management is not intuitive initially, but the interfaces are clean and easy to look at, meaning that the inevtiable hours you'll spend staring at the console troubleshooting permissions or errors feels that little bit less tedious.
author: Connor Q.
pros: It provides direct access to a plethora of enterprise services and solutions in a centralised console that would have traditionally required enormous investment of time, resources and expertise to be able to investigate and/or utilise. The management console has what seems to be an extremely deep learning curve, but if you are somewhat familiar with the way the services are delivered (i.e interactions and distinctions between IAM users; roles; groups; manager vs in-line policies etc) then it can begin to grow into a powerful tool. Once familiarised enough there are plenty of options and third party solutions that can be integrated and architected to automate and standardise on processes for service delivery which I think is where AWS really begins to excel.
cons: It is a gargantuan task to undertake with little or no experience, unless coming from another cloud provider potentially such as Azure or GCP. To that end you could spend a hundred hours on the platform and still find new services and errors that are completely new to you; when paired with frequent updates and changes to services, architecture and command formatting this can make managing in small or medium businesses a very daunting task. The use of third party tools such as Intruder (automatically scans your env for security concerns) feels like a baseline requirement for security and risk management.

author: Samuel H.
pros: It is comprehensive and full of information on all the resources in your account.
cons: The console across various services is inconsistent, making it frustrating at times.

description: It's been s good experience over all I don't have any complaints
author: Ashley B.
pros: The product is easy to understand corrects my mistakes. It's not frustrating it's simple
cons: Only the fact that my phone is crappy so it's very slow and the app with go off

review_title: Reliable cloud management tool with a steep learning curve
description: Overall my experience with the AWS Management Console has been really positive. It’s a powerful and reliable platform that gives me complete control over my cloud environment, whether I’m launching EC2 instances, managing S3 storage, or configuring IAM roles. Once you get familiar with it, the workflow feels efficient and the integration between services is seamless. That said, there’s definitely a learning curve. the number of options and depth of settings can be intimidating at first. But after spending some time with it, it becomes an indispensable tool for managing and monitoring everything in AWS.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like how everything I need is in one place. I can easily manage EC2 instances, S3 buckets, and IAM roles without having to jump between different tools. The interface has improved a lot over time and feels more intuitive now, with quick access to recently used services and a solid search bar that actually works well. I also like the reliability. it’s fast, stable, and gives me clear visibility into my resources and costs. Overall, it just makes managing AWS services much smoother once you get used to where everything is.
cons: it can feel a bit overwhelming at times, especially with so many services and settings packed into one place. Some sections, like IAM permissions or networking configurations, are not very intuitive and require a lot of clicks to get simple things done. The layout isn’t always consistent across services, so it sometimes feels like you’re using a bunch of different tools stitched together. It would be great if the console were a bit more streamlined and userfriendly, especially for everyday management tasks.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall experience would be positive. I don't have any experience with it so any page/tab I get to is my first time getting to it. It is easy to navigate, but their documentation is very detailed in teh event I need help with something.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We use the AWS Management Console to manage our various AWS services. It has a small learning curve, a lot of features, and Customer Support is great whenever it is needed.
cons: Nothing I can think of. It is easy to use and I never have any problems navigating to anything I need.

description: Overall I like to use AWS management console, it is all in one console to use all kinds of AWS features.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: AWS management console is the entrance for all the AWS features, you can easily search the feature you want to use, and you can also log tickets through the console.
cons: Sometimes the features after login is just little overwhelming and It is not clear when you need to setup 2FA or not for login.

description: Overall, I’m pretty happy with my experience using the AWS Management Console. The interface is clean, organized, and easy to navigate, making it simple to manage services, deploy resources, and monitor performance in one place. Most tasks like launching VMs, configuring storage, and adjusting settings can be done quickly without much hassle. While some advanced features require add-ons or extra costs, the console itself feels reliable, responsive, and user-friendly. It’s a solid platform that makes cloud management efficient and convenient.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use, fast VM creation, and blazing internet speeds. Reliable cloud for teams daily usage and collaboration!
cons: Good core services, but some features cost extra and require paid add-ons to fully utilize AWS today

review_title: Easy to scale up and down to meet demand
author: Tim K.
pros: Ease of configuring scalable architecture that supports the business needs. Excellent monitoring and automatic backup capabilities.
cons: There are some challenging user interfaces that aren't intuitive for beginners to acclimate to, which can be intimidating.

review_title: Extremely powerful and efficient for Cloud Management
description: Overall, my experience with AWS Management Console has been great, but it comes with a learning curve. Once you get the hang of it, it’s a powerful tool that makes managing cloud resources efficient.
author: Ravi K.
pros: I love how AWS Management Console makes managing cloud resources so much easier. The best part is how seamlessly all the AWS services connect, so I don’t have to jump between different tools.
cons: AWS Management Console has a very steep learning curve and might be very difficult for someone who is new, to get things to work. The interface might also be too much for a beginner to figure things out.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Highly satisfied with the usage of this cloud service. With a clear understanding of one's requirements and the right selection of AWS services, its full potential can be effectively harnessed
author: Shubham J.
pros: All services can be utilized in various combinations within a defined framework, seamlessly integrating to meet specific demands. Cost optimization is a key feature, allowing for fine-tuning of resource usage and tracking of underutilized resources. Also on-demand functionalities and pre-defined scaling of compute and storage resources further enhance cost efficiency.
cons: The cost forecast is somewhat inaccurate, it gives a tentative estimate. Deleting instances can be challenging, even with super admin roles. While cost management is possible, the pricing feels a bit steep.

description: Overall I think AWS is an industry leader due to the ability to make anything you want with their mass amount of services though it is not for the faint hearted and can come with a high learning curve and cost.
author: Torlock C.
pros: Breadth of services, customisable, good API.
cons: Complexity is high though there are plenty of good resources and documentation. Cost can also be high depending on the requirements.

description: I always find what I need within the console fairly quickly, and it works quite smooth with good loading speeds.
author: Sean H.
pros: It has all of the options that you need, categorised as I would expected, with a great search function.
cons: Maybe it could be more tabulated rather than side menus

description: My experience was great i would recomend others to use AWS
author: Cherryl N.
pros: It is easily to be used and user friendly information is accessible easily
cons: The option i was given for my services was to confusing for me to choose considering i was a new comer.

author: Brittany M.
pros: easy-to-use interface that allows you to manage your resources through a web browser.
cons: In AWS it seems you are stuck managing ARNs for every damn object if you want to have anything less than a free-for-all in the account. This is an incredibly intense level of bureaucracy. I can see how the project abstraction could break down for a proper enterprise, which might really need that arbitrarily complex spaghetti of individual objects connected to individual objects
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Powerful Interface for Cloud Management: Good Features, Performance, and Usability
author: ISHWARI P.
pros: 1) Searching tools are very helpful to find out different tools helps to quickly access resources it saves time. 2)We can customize dashboards of console it helps in improving workflow efficiency. 3)Easier to handle for new user because of Navigators.
cons: 1) Different services and tools are available sometimes for new user its harder to navigate to next step. 2)Number of resources are available so sometimes may take larger time to load.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Dhakadiya O.
pros: Our comapny manages large number of real estate properties or in a say we have to keep track of large number of properties it gets really easy keeping all the high quality picture of them in once place as from amazon S3 which comes with aws management console.
cons: I sometimes felt complexities learing about it from IT guys and its cost can be bit complex to understand.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The documentation was apt. The UI was friendly for a first time user
author: George M.
pros: the very simple and friendly UI. I had no issues navigating the various features
cons: None yet. I enjoyed using AWS. I was a beginner and AWS was not intimidating

review_title: Great system to use if you're willing to learn.
description: Although it is not very beginner friendly, they offer free learning guides for everything. It will just require to spend some time get used to the system.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: AWS is feature packed management system for web based applications and servers.
cons: There is a steep learning curve getting the fundamentals and utilizing all the features of the software.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: AWS Management Console is my go-to tool for managing my cloud resources efficiently.
author: Laura T.
pros: The user-friendly interface makes it easy to navigate through different services and set up new resources.
cons: Sometimes the loading times can be slow, especially when working with large amounts of data.

description: Overall the experience with AWS Management Console has been good
author: Donovan V.
pros: I like the interface and UX of AWS Management Console the most
cons: There are a lot of cloud services and these types of services in general out there to use, so some increased updates and differentiation would be good moving forward.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: AWS Management Console gives me easy and quick access
author: Ram N.
pros: AWS Management Console offers easy-to-access panels. I can easily access my favorite AWS resources and jump to my most frequently used pages quickly.
cons: I wish there were more widgets. They could add widgets to show AWS down/up status for a particular region. Also, another widget to easily go to a particular specialized area such as networking, performance, Machine learning, and so on for quick access for each specialization area.

review_title: The console that does it all
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: One stop shop that's well organized to help navigate all of numerous items of training, deployment, support and more from AWS. The search functionailty allows me to quickly and easily find whatever I need if I forget where it's embedded in navigation.
cons: There is a lot to take in. As a newcomber, it can seem overwhelming, which is why getting even the most basic training is important to get any user started and not feel overwhelmed.

description: I've been using AWS Management Console for a few months and it's been really useful, the permissions/roles system is exactly what I needed. I barely experienced bad optimization on the dashboard, even when using the filter/search feature, I got the results pretty fast.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: AWS Management Console has all necessary features to manage your projects, it has a fast, optimized and userfriendly dashboard. Integrating it with other AWS was easy, with no issues.
cons: Its pricing could be considered a little expensive, specially when you need to use high resources. And some features are somewhat confusing.
